zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton於1330年出生在英國英格蘭巴金哈姆爾特易福德時，他的父親Sir John de Seyton16歲，母親Bridget Bassett26歲。他和Joan De Longueville至少有3個兒子和3個女兒。他去世於1396年在以色列מחוז ירושלים享年66歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton于1330年出生在英国英格兰巴金哈姆爾特易福德时，他的父亲Sir John de Seyton16岁，母亲Bridget Bassett26岁。他和Joan De Longueville至少有3个儿子和3个女儿。他去世于1396年在以色列מחוז ירושלים享年66岁。

English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

variants.name: John
meanings: English and Welsh: ultimately from the Hebrew personal name Yoḥanan ‘Jehovah has favored (me with a son)’ or ‘may Jehovah favor (this child)’. This personal name was adopted into Latin (via Greek) as Johannes, and has enjoyed enormous popularity in Europe throughout the Christian era, being given in honor of Saint John the Baptist, precursor of Christ, and of Saint John the Evangelist, author of the fourth gospel, as well as the nearly one thousand other Christian saints who bore the name. Some of the principal forms of the personal name in other languages are: Welsh Ieuan, Evan, Siôn, and Ioan; Scottish Ia(i)n; Irish Séan; German Johann, Johannes; Dutch and Slavic Jan; French Jean; Italian Giovanni; Spanish Juan; Portuguese João; Greek Iōannēs (vernacular Giannis, Yannis); Russian Ivan. There were a number of different forms of the name in Middle English, including Jan(e), a male name (see Jayne ); Jen (see Jenkin ); Jon(e) (see Jones ); and Han(n) (see Hann ). By the beginning of the 14th century John rivalled William in popularity and has always been a favorite name. Johan became Jo(h)n, and another Old French form Jehan was shortened to Jan and Jen, giving rise to Old French and Middle English diminutives such as Jonin, Janin, and Jenin. More common in Middle English were Jankin, Jonkin, and Jenkin, which were Middle Dutch pet forms introduced after the Conquest by Flemish and Picard settlers. The most common pet form of John was Jack, another borrowing from Flemish and Picard usage. Han may sometimes have been a short form of Johan but was more usually a pet form of Henry. There were also various Middle English feminine versions of this name (e.g. Joan, Jehan), some of which were indistinguishable from masculine forms. The distinction on grounds of gender between John and Joan was not firmly established in English until the 17th century. It was even later that Jean and Jane were specialized as specifically female names in English; bearers of these surnames and their derivatives are more likely to derive them from a male ancestor than a female. As a surname in the British Isles, John is particularly frequent in Wales, where it is a late formation representing Welsh Siôn rather than the older form Ieuan (which gave rise to the surname Evan ). In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. Assyrian/Chaldean Youkhana , French Jean , Hungarian János (see Janos ), Slovenian Janež and Janeš (see Janes ), Czech Jan , Albanian Gjoni , and their derivatives (see examples at Johnson ). The name John is also found among Christians in southern India (compare Ninan and Yohannan ), but since South Indians traditionally do not have hereditary surnames, the southern Indian name was in most cases registered as such only after immigration of its bearers to the US.
German: from a North German and Silesian variant of the personal name   Johannes  . This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare   Yohn  .

variants.name: Jean
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Pierre, Francois, Marcel, Yves, Andre, Jacques, Georges, Michel, Philippe, Serge, Yvon, Cecile.

French, Walloon, West Indian (mainly Haiti), Mauritian, and English (of French Huguenot origin): from the male personal name Jean, French form of John . This is one of the most frequent surnames in Haiti (along with Joseph and Pierre ). It is also established elsewhere in the West Indies (e.g. in the Dominican Republic, Saint Lucia, Saint Martin, and Bahamas).
English: habitational name for someone from Genoa in Italy; compare   Janeway  .

nameMeanings.name: Liz”
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Galician: habitational name from Liz in Lugo province, Galicia (Spain). As a Hispanic name it may also be an altered form of   Lis  . Compare   Deliz  .

variants.name: Lis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Stanislaw, Wladyslaw, Andrzej, Casimir, Ignatius, Malgorzata, Slawomir, Tadeusz, Tomasz, Boleslaw, Bronislaw, Darek.

Polish, Czech, Sorbian, and Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Polish and Czech dialect lis ‘fox’, Upper Sorbian lis ‘he-fox’, a nickname for a cunning person or, in the case of the Jewish name, an artificial name. The presumably cognate Sorbian surname Liš is found in Germanized and probably also Americanized forms, only (see Liesch and Lish ). Compare also Lese , Lies , and Liss .
Czech: nickname for a man with a bald pate, from a variant of the adjective lysý ‘bald’.

nameMeanings.name: Seyton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of   Deighton  .

variants.name: Deighton
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Durham): habitational name from any of several places in Yorkshire named Deighton, from Old English dīc ‘ditch, dike’ + tūn ‘settlement, enclosure’. See also Ditton .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament: ‘God is gracious’. See John .

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton was born in 1330, in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir John de Seyton, was 16 and his mother, Bridget Bassett, was 26. He had at least 3 sons and 3 daughters with Joan De Longueville. He died in 1396, in Jerusalem, Israel, at the age of 66.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton est né en 1330, à Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir John de Seyton, avait 16 ans et sa mère, Bridget Bassett, avait 26 ans. Il a eu au moins 3 fils et 3 filles avec Joan De Longueville. Il est décédé en 1396, à Jerusalem, Israël, à l’âge de 66 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton 1330 in Twyford, Buckinghamshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir John de Seyton 16 und seine Mutter Bridget Bassett 26. Er hatte mit Joan De Longueville mindestens 3 Söhne und 3 Töchter. Er starb 1396 im Alter von 66 Jahren in Jerusalem, Israel.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton è nato il nel 1330 nel Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Sir John de Seyton, aveva 16 anni e il suo madre, Bridget Bassett, aveva 26 anni. Lui avuto almeno 3 figli e 3 figlie con Joan De Longueville. Morì nel 1396, nel Jerusalem, Israele, all'età di 66 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir John "De St. Liz” Seytonが1330年にイギリスイギリスブクインガムシュアイアトウイフォードで生まれた時，父Sir John de Seytonは16歳，母Bridget Bassettは26歳であった。彼はJoan De Longuevilleとの間に少なくとも3人の息子と3人の娘をもうけた。彼は1396年にイスラエルמחוז ירושליםで66歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton(이)가 1330년 영국 잉글랜드 벅잉함실르 트w이퍼드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir John de Seyton(은)는 16세였고, 그의 어머니 Bridget Bassett(은)는 26세였다. 그는 Joan De Longueville(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 세 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1396년 이스라엘 מחוז ירושלים에서 66세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton nasceu em 1330, em Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Sir John de Seyton, tinha 16 anos e sua mãe, Bridget Bassett, tinha 26 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 3 filhos e 3 filhas com Joan De Longueville. Ele faleceu em 1396, em Jerusalem, Israel, com 66 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton родился в 1330 г. в местности твифорд, буккингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Sir John de Seyton было 16 лет, а матери, которую звали Bridget Bassett, 26. У него было по крайней мере 3 сына и 3 дочери от Joan De Longueville. Он умер в 1396 г. в местности מחוז ירושלים, Израиль в возрасте 66 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir John "De St. Liz” Seyton nació en 1330, en Twyford, Buckinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Sir John de Seyton, tenía 16 años y su mamá, Bridget Bassett, tenía 26 años. Tuvo por lo menos 3 hijos y 3 hijas con Joan De Longueville. Murió en 1396, en Jerusalem, Israel, a la edad de 66 años.
